title: Define Project Context for the PID
summary: Outlines the essential elements to include in the context section of a Project Initiation Document, covering business drivers, problem statement, project purpose, motivations, business goals, success criteria, and key performance indicators.
content: Context includes business drivers, problem statement, project purpose, technical vs. strategic motivations, business goals, success definition, and key metrics. A high‑level mission statement aligns the team and helps later decisions about scope changes.
can_answer: What information belongs in the context section of a PID?
How should business goals be documented in a PID?
How do I define success criteria and metrics for a project?
examples: Business goal: Increase online sales by 15% within 12 months.
Metric: Conversion rate improvement measured via Google Analytics.
warnings: Vague or missing context can cause misaligned expectations later.
